=========
vrage_api
=========


    Space Engineers Dedicated Server Remote API in Python


This module is essentially a python wrapper around the `requests` module to interact with the Space Engineers Dedicated Server Remote API. This module is (will) also be used in a server management tool which provides insight to your server(s). Currently if you run the GUI tool, you can't get any insight into players that have logged on and played on the server, performance over a period of time, chat logs, existing planets/asteroids and floating objects for example.

Once you have installed the package, you can view some of the example commands in the [examples](./examples/) folder. The example [commands.py](./examples/commands.py) will load a .env file in the root folder, so you can copy the `.env-example` as `.env` and fill out the information as required. All non 'get' commands will require the `--data` argument to pass a variable such as EntityId or SteamID. Ensure that you install argparse if you want to test out the commands using this script `pip install argparse`.

Function endpoint data
======================

GET
-------------------

`get_players <./examples/get/players.json>` - Gathers data about all the players such as: SteamID, Display Name, Faction, Faction Level and Ping

`get_asteroids <./examples/get/asteroids.json>` - Gathers data about all the asteroids in the current session such as: EntityId, position and display name

`get_floating_objects <./examples/get/floating_objects.json>` - Gathers data about all the floating objects that exist in the current session such as: EntityId, Kind, Position, Mass and Speed

`get_grids <./exmaples/get/grids.json>` - Gathers data about all the grids that exist in the current session such as: EntityId, Display Name, Position, Mass, Owner SteamID, total amount of PCU, powered status, etc...

`get_planets <./examples/get/planets.json>` - Gathers data about all the planets that exist in the current session such as: EntityId, Display Name and Position

`get_chat <./examples/get/chat.json>` - Gathers data about all the chat messages in the current session such as: SteamID, Display Name, Content (Message) and Timestamp

`get_server_info <./examples/get/server_info.json>` - Gathers data about the server such as: ServerId, Server Name, Total uptime, CPU load, World Name, Version, total amount of Players, total used PCU etc...

`get_server_ping<./examples/get/server_ping.json>` - Simple healthcheck for the server that responds "Pong" if successful.

`get_banned_players <./examples/get/banned_players.json>` - Gathers data about all banned players such as:

`get_kicked_players <./examples/get/kicked_players.json>`

DELETE / BAN / KICK
-------------------

All delete endpoints will typically just return a HTTP status code of 200 even if the EntityID doesn't exist...::

    {
        "meta": {
            "apiVersion": "1.0",
            "queryTime": 0.1115
        }
    }

So some care is required if you really want to identify for example, if a player has actually been kicked/banned properly based on gathering the data after performing a specific action.

A centralized API for multiple servers (and ensuring things like log history, player history) will be linked here directly once developed.

How to use
======================

    from vrage_api.vrage_api import VRageAPI

    api = VRageAPI(url="http://localhost:8080", token="my-secret-token")
    players = api.get_players()

    for player in players["data"]["Players"]:
        print(player["SteamID"], player["DisplayName"])

        if player["SteamID"] == 12341234567856780:
            api.player_ban(steam_id=12341234567856780)

.. _pyscaffold-notes:

Note
====

information on PyScaffold see https://pyscaffold.org/.

classes.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.build_headers
documentation:
Returns the relevant headers required to query
the Space Engineers VRage API. Terrible API implementation from Keen Software house...

Arguments:
    endpoint:   URL endpoint (eg. /vrageremote/v1/server)

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.query
documentation:
Builds a GET HTTP message

Arguments:
    endpoint:   URL endpoint (eg. /server/ping)

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_players
documentation: Gets all players in the current session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_asteroids
documentation: Gets all the asteroids in the current session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_floating_objects
documentation: Gets all floating objects in the current session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_grids
documentation: Gets all the grids in the current session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_planets
documentation: Gets all the planets in the current session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_chat
documentation: Gets the chat history for the current session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_server_info
documentation: Gets Server Info like used PCU stats, Server Name, CPU Load, etc..

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_server_ping
documentation: Gets Server Ping (eg. if server is up and responding)

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_banned_players
documentation: Gets all banned players for the server

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.get_kicked_players
documentation: Gets all kicked players for the current session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.delete_asteroid
documentation:
Deletes an asteroid in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.delete_floating_object
documentation:
Deletes a floating object in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.delete_grid
documentation:
Deletes a grid in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.delete_planet
documentation:
Deletes a planet in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.stop_grid
documentation:
Stops a moving grid in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.stop_floating_object
documentation:
Stops a floating object in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.power_down_powered_grid
documentation:
Powers down a grid in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.power_up_powered_grid
documentation:
Powers up a grid in the current session

Arguments:
    entity_id:  ID of the entity

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.stop_server
documentation: Stops the current server session

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.player_promote
documentation:
Promotes a player up 1 level based on the SteamID

Arguments:
    steam_id:   SteamID of the player

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.player_demote
documentation:
Demotes a player down 1 level based on the SteamID

Arguments:
    steam_id:   SteamID of the player

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.player_ban
documentation:
Ban a player based on the SteamID

Arguments:
    steam_id:   SteamID of the player

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.player_unban
documentation:
Unban a player based on the SteamID

Arguments:
    steam_id:   SteamID of the player

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.player_kick
documentation:
Kick a player based on the SteamID

Arguments:
    steam_id:   SteamID of the player

methods.name: vrage_api.vrage_api.VRageAPI.player_unkick
documentation:
Unkick a player based on the SteamID

Arguments:
    steam_id:   SteamID of the player

A simple wrapper that uses the request module to build
the correct payload and headers to query the Space Engineers
Server API

Arguments:
    url:        Base URL (eg. http://<your-ip>:8080)
    token:      API Token found in the configuration GUI/XML
